contents.value:
```rust
macro_rules! println
```
Prints to the standard output, with a newline. Uses the `format!` syntax. Panics if writing to `io::stdout()` fails.

```rust
macro_rules! eprintln
```
Prints to the standard error, with a newline. Uses the `format!` syntax. Panics if writing to `io::stderr()` fails.

```rust
macro_rules! format
```
Creates a `String` using interpolation of runtime expressions. The first argument is a format string, using the same syntax as `println!`.

```rust
macro_rules! vec
```
Creates a `Vec` containing the arguments. Supports two forms: `vec![a, b, c]` creates a vector with those elements, and `vec![val; n]` creates a vector of `n` copies of `val`.

```rust
macro_rules! panic
```
Panics the current thread. Can be called with a formatting string and arguments or with a payload of any type that implements `Any + Send`.

```rust
macro_rules! assert
```
Asserts that a boolean expression is true at runtime. If the expression is false, `panic!` is called with an optional custom message.

```rust
macro_rules! assert_eq
```
Asserts that two expressions are equal to each other (using `PartialEq`). On failure, prints the values of both expressions.

```rust
macro_rules! assert_ne
```
Asserts that two expressions are not equal to each other (using `PartialEq`). On failure, prints the values of both expressions.

```rust
macro_rules! todo
```
Indicates unfinished code. Panics with "not yet implemented" message. Useful as a placeholder during development.

```rust
macro_rules! unimplemented
```
Indicates unimplemented code by panicking with "not implemented". Use `todo!` for code you intend to write later.

```rust
macro_rules! dbg
```
Prints and returns the value of a given expression to stderr for quick debugging. Prints the file, line number, expression, and value.

```rust
macro_rules! write
```
Writes formatted data into a buffer. The buffer must implement `std::fmt::Write` or `std::io::Write`. Returns `fmt::Result`.

```rust
macro_rules! writeln
```
Writes formatted data with a newline into a buffer. Same as `write!` but appends `\n`.

```rust
macro_rules! include_str
```
Includes a UTF-8 encoded file as a string slice (`&'static str`) at compile time. The file path is relative to the current file.

```rust
macro_rules! include_bytes
```
Includes a file as a byte array reference (`&'static [u8; N]`) at compile time. The file path is relative to the current file.

```rust
macro_rules! concat
```
Concatenates literals into a `&'static str`. The arguments must be literals (string, numeric, char, or boolean).

```rust
macro_rules! cfg
```
Evaluates configuration flags at compile time. Returns `true` if the configuration predicate evaluates to true.

```rust
macro_rules! env
```
Inspects an environment variable at compile time. Produces a compile error if the variable is not set. Returns `&'static str`.

```rust
macro_rules! matches
```
Returns a `bool` indicating whether an expression matches any of the given patterns. Equivalent to a `match` with `true`/`false` arms.

```rust
macro_rules! compile_error
```
Causes compilation to fail with the given error message when encountered. Useful in `cfg` blocks and procedural macros.

```rust
pub struct String
```
A UTF-8–encoded, growable string. It is the most common string type in Rust and owns its contents. Created via `String::new()`, `String::from()`, or `.to_string()`.

```rust
pub struct Vec<T>
```
A contiguous growable array type, written as `Vec<T>`. Provides O(1) indexed access, amortized O(1) push, and O(n) insert/remove.

```rust
pub struct HashMap<K, V, S = RandomState>
```
A hash map implemented with quadratic probing and SIMD lookup. Requires keys to implement `Eq` and `Hash`.

```rust
pub struct HashSet<T, S = RandomState>
```
A hash set implemented as a `HashMap` where the value is `()`. Provides fast membership testing.

```rust
pub struct BTreeMap<K, V>
```
An ordered map based on a B-Tree. Keys must implement `Ord`. Provides O(log n) lookup, insertion, and removal.

```rust
pub enum Option<T> {
    None,
    Some(T),
}
```
Represents an optional value: every `Option` is either `Some` and contains a value, or `None`. Used extensively in Rust for nullable values.

```rust
pub enum Result<T, E> {
    Ok(T),
    Err(E),
}
```
A type for returning and propagating errors. `Ok(T)` represents success, `Err(E)` represents failure. Use the `?` operator to propagate errors.

```rust
pub struct Box<T: ?Sized>
```
A pointer type for heap allocation. `Box<T>` provides ownership of a heap-allocated value of type `T`. When a box goes out of scope, its destructor is called and the heap memory is freed.

```rust
pub struct Rc<T: ?Sized>
```
A single-threaded reference-counting pointer. `Rc` provides shared ownership of a value. Cloning produces a new pointer to the same allocation. The value is dropped when the last `Rc` is dropped.

```rust
pub struct Arc<T: ?Sized>
```
A thread-safe reference-counting pointer. `Arc` stands for Atomically Reference Counted. It provides shared ownership across threads. Like `Rc`, but uses atomic operations for thread safety.

```rust
pub struct Mutex<T: ?Sized>
```
A mutual exclusion primitive for protecting shared data. Each mutex has a type parameter `T` which is the type being protected. The data can only be accessed through the RAII guard returned from `lock()`.

```rust
pub struct Cell<T: ?Sized>
```
A mutable memory location for `Copy` types. Provides interior mutability by moving values in and out of the `Cell`. Not thread-safe.

```rust
pub struct RefCell<T: ?Sized>
```
A mutable memory location with dynamically checked borrow rules. Provides interior mutability with runtime borrow checking. Panics on conflicting borrows.

```rust
pub trait Copy: Clone { }
```
Types whose values can be duplicated simply by copying bits. Types that implement `Copy` are implicitly copied on assignment. Cannot be implemented for types containing heap-allocated data.

```rust
pub trait Eq: PartialEq<Self> { }
```
Trait for equality comparisons which are equivalence relations. This is a marker trait that adds the guarantee of reflexivity to `PartialEq`.

```rust
pub unsafe auto trait Send { }
```
Types that can be transferred across thread boundaries. Most types are `Send`. Notable exceptions: `Rc<T>`, raw pointers.

```rust
pub unsafe auto trait Sync { }
```
Types for which it is safe to share references between threads. A type `T` is `Sync` if `&T` is `Send`. Notable exceptions: `Cell<T>`, `RefCell<T>`.
